pattern: Precomputing, for every position, how long the non-increasing run ending just before it and the non-decreasing run starting just after it are lets each index be judged in constant time.
hints: For each index you need two facts: is the block of k elements before it non-increasing, and is the block of k after it non-decreasing.
Sliding these checks naively per index is wasteful; precompute run lengths instead.
Build a left array of how far back the non-increasing streak reaches and a right array of the non-decreasing streak forward, then an index is good when both streaks are at least k.

edge_inputs.description: two good indices in the middle
k equals one any index in range qualifies trivially
k at half length leaves a thin valid range
all equal elements satisfy both monotonicities
strictly increasing breaks the non-increasing prefix
valley shape good at the bottom

stress.description: random array over a small value range with a valid k to create monotone streaks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n // 2)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: small value range makes equal/monotone streaks frequent
